rag_rules: Insert AGREE annexes into the target component implementation scope when bindings depend on subcomponent ports or implementation-level context.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL structure by only inserting AGREE annexes where the target component's scope permits reference to its direct ports and subcomponents.
Do not reference AADL properties or unit tokens as AGREE variables; only use explicitly exposed ports or constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax.
Implementation-level contracts should only reference ports and variables available in the implementation scope.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing component-level assumptions in implementation annexes unless they bind to implementation-specific subcomponent ports.
Never convert Boolean or opaque data ports into numeric range comparisons unless the AADL model declares them as numeric scalars.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
